competitive_position.summary: Zycus is the confirmed front-runner (Martin, 2026-06-15: 'you're a front runner'; ~99.8% RFP score). The strongest CURRENT threat is not a named rival but the SteerCo fairness re-score of thin-scope, lower-cost vendors plus 'do nothing/delay' against a tight close — both neutralised by locking a formal award. The lower-cost vendors are dismissed as functionally inadequate (~3 modules) and one failed to submit an implementation cost. GEP and Coupa are named in CRM as the field but were not actively pressed in recent calls; the buyer praised Zycus's Merlin Intake UI as the best they have seen.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Delay
competitors.quote: We want to make sure we do the right things... we are taking our time to make sure we get this right.
competitors.how_we_win: Anchor to the buyer's stated year-end go-live and Q3 implementation timeline to keep urgency.

competitors.name: Unnamed lower-cost S2P vendor (functionally thin, ~3 modules)
competitors.quote: Some are cheaper, but it looks like they've only got like 3 modules, which we don't think is gonna cover what we need at all.
competitors.how_we_win: Emphasize full-suite coverage (intake, sourcing, contracting, supplier risk) vs niche point solution that will require bolt-ons or future replacements. Highlight Teams-native guided intake and Risk Radar (sanctions, ESG, financials) as differentiators the buyer explicitly needs.

competitors.name: Unnamed S2P vendor (incomplete/non-responsive RFP)
competitors.quote: Martin: 'someone hasn't even put in an actual implementation cost yet... we're not trying too hard if they can't be bothered to do that' (2026-06-15 RFP clarification call).
competitors.how_we_win: Vendor self-disqualified by incomplete RFP response. No action required.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: GEP, Coupa - we have a better AI platform leading with Merlin Intake, in their words is the best UI they have seen.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin Intake agentic-AI UI advantage the buyer has already praised; reinforce full-suite parity.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: GEP, Coupa - we have a better AI platform leading with Merlin Intake.
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on agentic-AI and usability; not actively pressed in recent calls.

competitors.name: Lower-cost thin S2P vendor(s)
competitors.quote: Some are cheaper, but it's like it looks like they've only got like 3 modules, which we don't think is gonna cover what we need at all.
competitors.how_we_win: Hold the line on full-suite breadth and total cost of ownership; show that 3-module rivals cannot deliver the end-to-end guided journey Global Switch requires.

competitors.name: Non-responsive vendor (no implementation cost)
competitors.quote: Someone hasn't even put in an actual implementation cost yet... if they can't be bothered to do that, then no.
competitors.how_we_win: Already self-eliminating; emphasize Zycus's complete, costed proposal and delivery confidence.

champion_strength.summary: Martin Loveday is a strong, multi-threaded champion who authored the RFP, runs cadence, and is the access path to both budget (Anna-Marie) and the EB (Gavin); supported by senior sponsor Anna-Marie Ferguson and decision-maker Gavin Greer.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Two economic/budget authorities are mapped and not contradicted by the calls. CRM MEDDPICC 2.0 (updated 2026-06-16) names Gavin Greer (Group Procurement Director) as economic buyer and Anna-Marie Ferguson (Director of Business Intelligence and Performance) as budget owner. The calls corroborate Ferguson holds the commercial mandate: Anthony, 2026-05-27, 'Anna-Marie's given me the indication of what your budget is, so we're gonna be getting to that mark', and Martin, 2026-06-15, deferred the commercial sign-off to her ('Anna's gonna maybe come back on that specifically'). Gavin actively controlled scope and pricing decisions on the 2026-05-27 sizing call. The one gap a closer flags: Zycus has NOT had direct commercial closure with Ferguson on the final number — Martin relays her position rather than her engaging Zycus directly. In a champion-authored tender the path to her runs through Martin, not around him.

recommended_moves: Co-author a one-page mutual close plan with Martin that names a dated formal award by ~6 Jul and sequences redline + signing back from a target signature date.
Pre-stage the MSA redline pack now and offer a direct Zycus-legal-to-Shoosmiths call to trigger the moment the award lands.
Deliver a short-licence hands-on walkthrough for finance, legal and non-procurement users.
